label: smoking-gun-asymmetry-inverted
detail: WORKING spec-memory copy is the SIMPLE/permissive one; the two FAILING copies carry an extra canUnlinkExistingSocket() reclaim gate not present in spec-memory. Opposite of iter-1's hypothesis.

label: toctou-lstat-throws-enoent
detail: canUnlinkExistingSocket does fs.lstatSync(socketPath) with no ENOENT guard; if a racing sibling unlinked the socket between failed listenOnce and lstat, it throws ENOENT uncaught and the bind aborts.

label: ownership-realpath-refusal
detail: Even when socket exists, canUnlinkExistingSocket returns false (->throw err, bind fails) on allowed-root or uid mismatch; macOS /tmp->/private/tmp canonicalization drift under SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR=/tmp/<svc> makes the second daemon refuse to reclaim instead of rebinding.

label: dir-ownership-statSync-third-throw-site
detail: Failing copies add post-mkdir fs.statSync(socketDir) throwing on uid mismatch or group/world-writable mode; a uid/mode-drifted pre-existing /tmp/mk-* dir aborts the bind. Working copy has only mkdir(mode:0o700) with no post-check.

label: close-paths-identical
detail: close() teardown unlink arm is byte-identical across spec-memory and code-index; neither inode-guards the unlink, so close() cannot explain why only the two non-spec-memory daemons lost their listener.

label: racer-is-second-primary-not-secondary
detail: Secondaries report/bridge via launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs maybeBridgeLeaseHolder (action:report), they never run startIpcSocketServer; the EADDRINUSE racer is two owner-eligible primaries (trio recycle vs codex primary) racing the same /tmp/mk-<svc>/daemon-ipc.sock bind.

label: post-012-drift-confirmed
detail: 012 copied socket-server.ts verbatim with the permissive unlink-retry; canUnlinkExistingSocket + dir-ownership hardening (DR-008-01) was added to the two copies LATER (mtimes May29/Jun3) and never back-ported to spec-memory; 018 hardened only the spec-memory launcher and deferred owner-socket-path-in-lease (c).

label: live-state-reconfirmed
detail: Inodes 66749168 (code-index) / 66749225 (skill-advisor) / 66749238 (spec-memory) all srw------- @ Jun 4 18:35; lsof shows only spec-memory pid 61626 holds 3 FDs; code-index + skill-advisor sockets have NO FD holder.

label: bind-must-degrade-to-reclaim
detail: A benign stale unix socket (vanished or owned-by-us) must degrade to reclaim-and-rebind, never abort the bridge bind. spec-memory's permissive branch satisfies this; the two drifted copies violate it.

label: close-unlink-race-demoted
detail: iter-1 ranked-#1 close()-unlink race is NOT the differentiator: close() is identical across copies (F2-5). Divergence is the bind-time reclaim guard, not teardown.

label: secondary-launcher-races-bind-ruled-out
detail: Secondaries never bind; they report/bridge. The racer is a second primary (F2-6).

label: spec-memory-owner-fence-hypothesis-inverted
detail: The brief's hypothesized 'spec-memory has an owner-fence the others lack' is inverted: the OTHERS have the extra gate; spec-memory's ABSENCE of it is why spec-memory works.

label: toctou-window
detail: Race window = between this daemon's failed listenOnce (EADDRINUSE) and canUnlinkExistingSocket's lstat; a sibling unlink in that window throws ENOENT and aborts the bind.
